$comment: Machine-usable form of references/decision-catalog.md. Phase 1 loads this, prunes by project_type, skips brief-decided forks, and materializes one open_decision (or acceptance_criterion) pin per surviving fork. A frame, not a script — domain forks are added via the ledger's open taxonomy. Authoring source: decision-catalog.md; keep them in step.

clusters.title: Outcomes / acceptance criteria — roots of the DAG
clusters.elicit: the core use case as Given/When/Then statements with a verify hook
clusters.default_policy: smallest set of outcomes that delivers the core use case; the rest deferred

clusters.title: Product scope & domain model

options.label: Core entity set + v1 feature list (bounded)
options.implication: every unscoped entity is a deferred pin, not silent scaffolding

clusters.default_policy: smallest domain that delivers the core use case; everything else deferred

clusters.title: Persistence & data

options.label: Relational (Postgres/SQLite)
options.implication: schema-first; the DB schema is a source the contract derives from

clusters.default_policy: one relational datastore until a concrete need proves otherwise; schema-first

clusters.title: Identity & access
clusters.default_policy: third-party identity unless there's a reason to own it; single-tenant until a second tenant is real

clusters.title: API & contract (where the cross-layer contract lives)

options.label: RPC (tRPC/gRPC)
options.implication: typed calls; shared-types or protobuf carrier

options.label: GraphQL
options.implication: SDL is the carrier; resolver layer

clusters.default_policy: the lightest contract carrier that spans the languages in play; a shared-types package when the stack is TS end-to-end

clusters.title: Client & rendering
clusters.default_policy: server-render unless interactivity genuinely demands a SPA — client-heavy by exception, not default

clusters.title: Design system (DESIGN.md)

options.label: Author a DESIGN.md (palette + type ramp + radii tokens)
options.implication: impeccable detect enforces token-membership in CI; drift is a build failure, not a matter of taste

options.label: Mirror a component library's tokens into DESIGN.md
options.implication: the library's palette/type scale becomes the enforced contract

options.label: No formal design system in v1
options.implication: only universal a11y/slop checks run; design drift is unchecked by any contract

clusters.default_policy: author a minimal DESIGN.md from day one so design drift is a contract breach a CI check catches, not taste; skip only for headless/API-only

clusters.title: Sync & time

options.label: Background jobs/queue
options.implication: a queue + worker to run and observe

clusters.default_policy: synchronous request/response until a concrete latency or decoupling need justifies a queue; no event bus in v1

clusters.title: Boundaries & topology

options.label: Services
options.implication: network transport; per-service CI + contract tests

clusters.default_policy: modular monolith; split to services only when a module has a proven independent scaling or ownership need

clusters.title: Cross-cutting NFRs (elected as policies)
clusters.default_policy: validate at the contract boundary; structured errors from one taxonomy; Track-A tests on every decision-bearing slice; default-deny authz

clusters.title: Delivery & release
clusters.default_policy: the platform's boring default for the chosen topology; migrations versioned and expand/contract from commit one; every release rollback-ready

clusters.title: Operability (codebase-facing slice of operations)

options.label: Metrics
options.implication: a metrics sink the SLOs read

options.label: Signal manifest
options.implication: the physical anchor of every flip_signal

clusters.default_policy: structured logs + the few metrics the SLOs need; a signal manifest for every decision that carries a flip_signal
